steps.title: Count the number of doubling periods
steps.content:
From 1704 to 1944 is 1944 - 1704 = 240 years.
Since the population doubles every 60 years, the number of doubling periods is $$\frac{240}{60} = 4$$

steps.title: Set up the exponential growth equation
steps.content: When a population doubles every time period, after $n$ periods the population is: $$P = P_0 \cdot 2^n$$ where $P_0$ is the initial population. With 4 doublings: $$192,000 = P_0 \cdot 2^4$$

steps.title: Solve for the 1704 population
steps.content: Calculate $2^4 = 16$, then divide both sides by 16: $$P_0 = \frac{192,000}{16} = 12,000$$ The population in 1704 was 12,000.
